exemplar.title: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Clear, consistent links between each object and the IA prompt, demonstrating how knowledge transforms wonder.
Three distinct real-world contexts with specific examples (e.g., Fraunhofer lines in stellar spectroscopy, plate tectonics for earthquakes, activation-synthesis theory for dreams).
Justification of each object is grounded in evidence, with footnotes and references supporting the majority of claims.
Engaging cultural and metaphorical elements (Namazu myth, Red Thread of Fate, flame metaphor) that enrich the argument and reinforce the thesis.
weaknesses: Formatting issues: labeled as an essay rather than a visually framed exhibition and lacking tangible artefact images.
Citation inconsistencies and duplicated bibliography entries, with reliance on non-scholarly sources (Wikipedia, Medium).
Occasional repetition of the core idea (âtransforms rather than destroys wonderâ) and minor imprecision in some scientific claims.
Abrupt transitions between examples and introduction of new questions in the conclusion rather than synthesising previously discussed content.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the descriptors for the 5â6 band by clearly identifying three objects with specific real-world contexts and providing strong, evidence-based justifications linked directly to the IA prompt. Minor lapses in formatting, citation consistency, and occasional repetition prevent it from reaching the 7â8 band, but the overall clarity and depth of analysis warrant the top mark in the 5â6 range. A score of 6 accurately reflects this performance.

annotations.comment: The header labels this submission as an essay rather than an exhibition. Reframe the document clearly as an exhibition to align with the IA requirements.
The prompt âDoes acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder?â is restated clearly, centring the exhibition on the IA question from the start.
To meet exhibition criteria, identify three specific, tangible objects (with images) rather than conceptual examples, ensuring each objectâs real-world context is explicit.
This paragraph relies on broad claims about scientific materialism without referencing specific ToK sources or examples. Anchor generalisations with citations or concrete illustrations.
The distinction between subjective and objective wonder is promising but lacks support from ToK theory or philosophical sources. Cite a ToK theorist or philosopher to strengthen these definitions.
The thesis is clear: acquiring knowledge transforms rather than destroys wonder. This strong stance provides a focused lens for the exhibition.
The shift from dreams to the concept of love is abrupt. Add a linking sentence to improve flow and coherence between these two examples.
Consider displaying an fMRI image or dopamine system diagram to concretely represent the neuroscientific object on love.
The conclusion introduces new questions about wonder that were not discussed earlier. Ensure the conclusion synthesises rather than adds fresh content.
The summative language about âendless cycle of awe and inquiryâ powerfully reinforces the thesis that knowledge transforms wonder.
The contrast between ancient myths of the stars and modern spectroscopic discoveries is well contextualised, illustrating how scientific knowledge reshapes wonder.
To anchor this example in a tangible exhibition, consider including an image of a spectroscope or a Fraunhofer line chart to represent the first object.
The footnotes for Fraunhofer and Payne-Gaposchkin are formatted inconsistently and split across separate entries. Merge and standardise them to improve clarity.
The vivid description of Namazu and its seismological replacement by plate tectonics effectively contrasts myth and modern understanding.
The claim that the Earthâs crust consists of â13 or 14 enormous platesâ is vague. Provide the most up-to-date scientific consensus and cite a geoscience source.
To bring this into an exhibition format, include a seismograph image or a map of tectonic boundaries as a second object with its context.
The term âquantitiveâ is misspelled. Correct it to âquantitativeâ and ensure consistent terminology throughout.
The discussion of activation-synthesis theory and REM sleep is clear and effectively shows how scientific methods transform wonder in dreams.
Include an EEG scan or a dream-journal excerpt as a third object to provide a concrete artefact for the human-sciences example.
The cultural account of the Red Thread of Fate is engaging and illustrates how cultural myths fuel wonder in human relationships.
The phrase âthis driveâ is ambiguous in the final paragraph. Specify what âdriveâ refers to for greater clarity.
Reinforce the exhibition structure by restating each object and its context in the final statement rather than a general remark.
The flame metaphor effectively conveys the enduring nature of wonder, adding rhetorical strength to the conclusion.
The bibliography repeats the plate tectonics source twice. Remove duplicate entries to streamline references.
Explicitly list each of the three objects and their unique contributions in the conclusion to meet the exhibitionâs requirement for justification.
The reliance on Wikipedia and Medium sources weakens academic rigour. Replace these with peer-reviewed or authoritative publications.
Alphabetise entries and adopt a consistent citation style (APA or MLA) to enhance the professionalism of the bibliography.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content:

three objects, but the

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
